steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb linking "only Algerian authorities" to the state "permitted to make arrests there." The passage describes a current, ongoing rule about how authority works in Algiers — not a past situation. The blank must be a present-tense linking verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘were being’: Past progressive tense implies the authorization was happening at a specific moment in the past but is no longer true now. The passage describes a standing current rule. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘were’: Simple past tense signals the authorization ended at some point. The passage gives no indication the rule changed. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘had been’: Past perfect signals the authorization was completed before some other past event. No such sequence exists in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘are’: Simple present tense matches the passage's present-tense framing ("Interpol can help," "you won't see") and correctly expresses a current standing rule. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
